m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will be the output of the following code?
#include<iostream>
#include<iomanip>
using namespace std;
void CurrencyFormat(float amount) {
       cout << "Rs."<<std::setw(10)<<std::setprecision(3)<<std::setfill('*')<< amount;      
}
void main() {
       CurrencyFormat(56.6412f);
}
m_Item1.Explanation: setw() is manipulater function use for specifing width . setprecision() is use for set precision for number and setfill() function is use to fill unused places.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
ifstream infile;
infile.open (“data”);
In the above code, what is the default open mode for the file?
m_Item1.Explanation:
This data type represents the input file stream and is used to read information from files. 
so default mode for opening file for reading is ops::in

m_Item1.QuestionText:
int main(int argc, char * args[])
In the above statement what does argc means?
m_Item1.Explanation: argc (ARGument Count) is int and stores number of command-line arguments passed by the user including the name of the program. So if we pass a value to a program, value of argc would be 2 (one for argument and one for program name).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is TRUE about eof() member function of class ios?

Options.OptionText: It returns zero if end of file is encountered
It returns non-zero if end of file is encountered
It returns one if end of file is encountered

m_Item1.Explanation: eof() is used in file handling and it returns non-zero if end of file is encountered.

m_Item1.QuestionText: endl is a _____
m_Item1.Explanation: Manipulators are operators used in C++ for formatting output.  endl is the line feed operator in C++. It acts as a stream manipulator whose purpose is to feed the whole line and then point the cursor to the beginning of the next line.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What must be specified when we construct an object of class ostream?
m_Item1.Explanation: If you construct an object of class ostream, you must specify a streambuf object to the constructor.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The maximum combined length of the command-line arguments including the spaces between adjacent arguments is

Options.OptionText: It may vary from one operating system to another

m_Item1.Explanation: we can also pass arguments to the main function of C++ which are known as Command Line Arguments. Command line arguments are given after the name of the program during the execution of the program in a command-line shell. And length of command line arguments including the spaces vary from one operating system to another.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which stream class is used to only write on files ..
m_Item1.Explanation:
ofstream: Stream class to write on files
ifstream: Stream class to read from files
fstream: Stream class to both read and write from/to files.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of these is the correct statement about eof() ?

Options.OptionText: Returns true if a file open for reading has reached the next character.
Returns true if a file open for reading has reached the next word.
Returns true if a file open for reading has reached the end.
Returns true if a file open for reading has reached the middle.

m_Item1.Explanation: eof is used to read file till end of file so,this function returns true if a file open for reading purpose and reached to end of that file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods can be used to open a file in file handling?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax for file opening is
  • ofstream out("ABC.txt") //constructot method
  • ofstream out;
      out.open("ABC.txt");  //with open function.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which operator is used to insert the data into file?
m_Item1.Explanation: You can write information to a file from your program using the stream insertion operator <<

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is correct syntax ?
m_Item1.Explanation: open is fucntion so it must be access using dot(.) operator and ios is class hence out must be used with ::.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
#include <iostream>
#include <fstream>
#include <string>
using namespace std;
int main()
{
     ofstream ofile;
     ofile.open("find.txt");
     ofile << "letsfindcourse" << endl;
     cout << "Data written to file" << endl;
     ofile.close();
     return 0;
}

Options.OptionText: The program prints "letsfindcourse" in the file find.txt

m_Item1.Explanation: object of ofstream class use to open file for writing mode and ofile<< is use to write something in file so the program print or write letsifndcourse in file find.txt

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the code fout.open("scores.dat", ios::out);

Options.OptionText: ios::out is the stream operation mode.
foutis the name of the file.

m_Item1.Explanation:
ios::out means you want to open a file in the output mode (for writing data to a file)
ios::in means you want to open a file in the input mode (for extracting data in the file)
ios::app means you want to open a file in the append mode (for writing data to a file after the last byte.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which of the following classes, Streams that will be performing both input and output operations must be declared?
m_Item1.Explanation: A stream that will be be performing both input and output operations must be declared as class fstream.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which out of the following is return type of is_open() function.
m_Item1.Explanation: You can check if a file has been correctly opened by calling the member function is_open(): bool is_open(); that returns a bool type value indicating true in case that indeed the object has been correctly associated with an open file or false otherwise.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is used to position back from the end of file object?
m_Item1.Explanation: The member function seekg is used to position back from the end of file object.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will be the output of the program ?
#include<stdio.h>
int main(){
enum days {MON=-1, TUE, WED=6, THU, FRI,       SAT};
printf("%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d ", MON,TUE, WED, THU, FRI, SAT);
return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
MON is assigned -1.
TUE follows MON and is automatically assigned 0 (since it follows MON, which is -1).
WED is explicitly assigned 6.
THU follows WED and is automatically assigned 7 (since it follows WED, which is 6).
FRI follows THU and is automatically assigned 8 (since it follows THU, which is 7).
SAT follows FRI and is automatically assigned 9 (since it follows FRI, which is 8).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which header file has to be included in the C++ program which communicates with files?
Which header file is needed to perform file input and output operations in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'fstream' header file provides classes for both input and output file operations in C++.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the fstream class in C++?

Options.OptionText: To read and write to files

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'fstream' class is used for both reading from and writing to files in C++.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you open a file 'example.txt' for writing using ofstream?
m_Item1.Explanation: Using 'ofstream file('example.txt');' opens the file for writing by default.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which mode allows you to append data to the end of a file?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'ios::app' mode opens the file in append mode, allowing data to be added to the end of the file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will the following code do? ofstream file('data.txt', ios::app);

Options.OptionText: Create a new file or truncate it
Create a new file or append data to it
Create a new file only if it doesn't exist

m_Item1.Explanation: The code opens 'data.txt' and appends data if the file already exists or creates a new file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: To open a file in both reading and writing mode, which class should be used?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'fstream' class supports both input and output operations, making it suitable for reading and writing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you close a file stream in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: To close a file in C++, you use the 'close()' method on the file stream object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is used to set the position of the next character to be extracted from the input stream?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'seekg()' function is used with ifstream to set the position of the next character to be extracted.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default mode for opening a file using ofstream?
m_Item1.Explanation: The default mode for 'ofstream' is 'ios::out', which opens the file for writing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the expression 'file.open('example.txt', ios::app);', what does 'ios::app' signify?

Options.OptionText: Open the file for input and output
Open the file in binary mode
Open the file and append to it

m_Item1.Explanation: 'ios::app' is a file mode that opens the file in append mode, adding data to the end.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you read from a file using ifstream?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '>>' operator is typically used to extract data from an ifstream object in C++.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When a file is opened in ios::trunc mode, what happens?

Options.OptionText: File is truncated to zero length

m_Item1.Explanation: 'ios::trunc' mode clears the existing file content, truncating it to zero length.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will happen if you try to open a file using ifstream and it does not exist?
m_Item1.Explanation: If the file doesn't exist when opening with ifstream, the 'failbit' is set, indicating failure.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is used to check if the end of a file has been reached in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'eof()' function checks whether the end of file has been reached during reading.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the use of the 'tellg()' function in file handling?

Options.OptionText: To return the current write position
To change the current write position
To return the current get position
To change the current get position

m_Item1.Explanation: 'tellg()' returns the current position in the input stream, specifically the get position.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which file mode should be used with ofstream to write to a new file or truncate an existing one?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'ios::trunc' is used to open a file for writing, clearing existing contents if any.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default mode for opening a file using ifstream?
m_Item1.Explanation: The default mode for 'ifstream' is 'ios::in', which opens the file for reading.

m_Item1.QuestionText: To copy the binary data from one file to another, which of the following modes should be used?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'ios::binary' mode treats the file as a binary file, which is necessary for copying binary data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the use of the 'flush()' member function in file handling?

Options.OptionText: To synchronize the buffer with the file

m_Item1.Explanation: 'flush()' is used to clear the output buffer, ensuring that all output data is written to the file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method would check if a file has been successfully opened?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'is_open()' method checks if a file stream is associated with an open file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the standard stream for input operations in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'cin' object is the standard stream in C++ used for input operations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which header file is needed for using 'cout' in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'cout' object is declared in the <iostream> header file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which operator is used with 'cout' to send output to the console?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '<<' operator is used with 'cout' to send output to the console.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What symbol is used to separate multiple values being output by 'cout'?
m_Item1.Explanation: Multiple values can be separated by the '<<' operator in a 'cout' statement.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you flush the output buffer in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: Using 'endl' not only adds a newline character but also flushes the output buffer.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of these is a correct way to read a string with spaces using 'cin'?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'cin.getline(str, size)' is used to read a line of text including spaces into 'str'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function can be used to read a single character from the console?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'cin.get()' reads a single character from the input.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will be the output of: cout << 'A' << 1 << 'B';?
m_Item1.Explanation: The values are output in the order of 'A', '1', 'B', so the correct output is 'A1B'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which object is used for logging error messages in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'cerr' object is used for writing error messages to the standard error stream.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will be the result of: cout << 5/2;
m_Item1.Explanation: In C++, integer division results in an integer. Therefore, 5 divided by 2 is 2.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the role of 'clog' in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'clog' is used for sending errors and log messages with buffering.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which manipulator is used to change the format of output in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'setw' is a manipulator used to set the width of the output field in C++.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the output of: cout << 'Hello' << endl << 'World';
m_Item1.Explanation: 'endl' moves the cursor to the next line, so the output will be 'Hello\nWorld'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you skip whitespace characters with 'cin' for a single string input?
m_Item1.Explanation: By default, 'cin' skips whitespace characters for standard input.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which manipulators are used by default for floating point numbers in 'cout'?
m_Item1.Explanation: By default, floating-point numbers are output in scientific or fixed format as needed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you ensure 'cout' always output floating-point numbers with a fixed number of decimal places?
m_Item1.Explanation: To output floating-point numbers with a fixed number of decimals, use 'setf(ios::fixed)' along with 'setprecision(n)'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the function of 'noskipws' in C++?

Options.OptionText: Do not skip white spaces in input
Remove white spaces in a string

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'noskipws' manipulator is used with 'cin' and it ensures white spaces are not skipped.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which stream object is used for buffered standard output in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'cout' is the stream object used for buffered standard output in C++.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In C++, what will be the effect of using 'cin.ignore()'?

Options.OptionText: Ignores certain number of characters from input

m_Item1.Explanation: 'cin.ignore()' can be used to skip over a number of characters in the input buffer.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the result when cout is used with a boolean variable without using boolalpha?
m_Item1.Explanation: Without 'boolalpha', boolean variables are output as integers: 1 for true and 0 for false.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which manipulator is used to fix the number of decimal places to be displayed in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'fixed' manipulator is used in conjunction with 'setprecision' to set the number of decimal places for floating-point numbers to be displayed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the output of the code snippet: cout << setw(5) << 42 << endl;?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'setw' manipulator sets the width of the output field. Since '42' is two digits, three spaces precede it to fill the width.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following manipulators forces the output to display in scientific notation?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'scientific' manipulator is used to display floating-point numbers in scientific notation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When using std::setw() with the std::cout stream, where does the width affect the output?

Options.OptionText: On the next output item after std::setw()

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'setw' manipulator affects the next output field immediately following it.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the manipulator std::setprecision() alter when used with floating-point numbers?

Options.OptionText: Number of digits after the decimal

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'setprecision' manipulator specifies the total number of significant digits by default.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement best describes the effect of std::left on output order?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'left' manipulator causes output to be left-aligned in the specified field width.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does the std::showbase manipulator affect integer output?

Options.OptionText: Always displays the '0x' prefix for hexadecimal numbers
Displays positive sign for all integers
Displays base prefix for binary and octal also

m_Item1.Explanation: 'showbase' displays the base prefix for integers, such as '0x' for hexadecimal numbers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a non-parametric manipulator in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: Non-parametric manipulators like 'fixed' don't take arguments, whereas 'setw' and 'setprecision' do.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which manipulator is typically used to fill width with a specific character in output?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'setfill' is used to specify the character used to fill the output field width, often used with 'setw'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Given the code snippet: cout << hex << 100 << endl;, what will be the output?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'hex' manipulator changes the integer to hexadecimal representation; 100 decimal is 64 in hexadecimal.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the std::noshowpoint manipulator?

Options.OptionText: Prevents display of decimal point for floating-point numbers
Hides plus sign for positive numbers

m_Item1.Explanation: 'noshowpoint' ensures that the decimal point is not shown for floating-point numbers with no fractional part.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will be the output of this code: cout << hex << showbase << 100 << endl;?
m_Item1.Explanation: 'showbase' displays the '0x' prefix for hexadecimal numbers, and 100 in decimal converts to '64' in hexadecimal.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which library in C++ provides manipulators like setw, setprecision, and hex?
m_Item1.Explanation: The iomanip library provides parametric manipulators such as 'setw', 'setprecision', and others.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which manipulator ensures that numeric values are aligned to the right?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'right' manipulator makes sure numbers are right-aligned in their output field.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does std::uppercase modify the output stream?

Options.OptionText: Only affects hexadecimal and scientific notations
Has no effect on numeric output

m_Item1.Explanation: 'uppercase' modifies letters in hexadecimal numbers and the letter 'e' in scientific notation to uppercase.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default alignment of output in C++ before any manipulators are applied?
m_Item1.Explanation: By default, text and numeric output are left-aligned unless other manipulators like 'right' are used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the std::setfill manipulator?

Options.OptionText: It changes the fill character used in setw
It sets the precision of floating-point numbers
It aligns text to the center
It sets the number of base characters displayed

m_Item1.Explanation: 'setfill' specifies the character to use for padding when fields are of a larger width, often used with 'setw'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which manipulator is used to convert all alphabetic characters to lowercase in output?
m_Item1.Explanation: C++ does not have a 'lowercase' manipulator. Lowercase transformation needs to be done programmatically.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the use of std::internal manipulator?

Options.OptionText: Aligns sign adjacent to the number

m_Item1.Explanation: 'internal' alignment places any sign character inside the padding, adjacent to the number itself.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does the std::flush manipulator affect an output stream?

Options.OptionText: It forces all characters in buffer to be output

m_Item1.Explanation: 'flush' manipulator forces the buffer content to be written to the output stream immediately.
